points.text: Monte Rosa Therapeutics presented preclinical data for MRT-55811, a first-in-class CCNE1-directed molecular glue degrader, showing deep tumor regressions in CCNE1-amplified ovarian, breast, and gastric cancer in vivo models.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics reported MRT-55811 exhibited superior selectivity over clinical-stage CDK2 inhibitors with no detectable degradation of closely related cyclins or CDKs, supporting an IND submission later in 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — MRT-55811 — CCNE1-amplified solid tumors — First-in-class molecular glue degrader induced deep tumor regressions in ovarian, breast, and gastric cancer models; IND submission planned for late 2026.

Monte Rosa Therapeutics — Clinical-stage molecular glue degrader development — MRT-55811 (CCNE1-directed MGD) — Preclinical data presented at AACR 2026; QuEEN™ discovery engine utilized to target undruggable CCNE1.

MRT-55811 — Mechanism: Molecular glue degrader targeting CCNE1 — Phase: Preclinical — Population: CCNE1-amplified solid tumors (ovarian, breast, gastric) — Comparator: CDK2 inhibitors — Results: Deep tumor regression; superior selectivity with no off-target cyclin/CDK degradation.
